currentFocus: Accepted coordination policy/state integration is being closed out so the next executable baton can start from main on repo-quality, modularity, and stale-doc cleanup.

currentTruth.publicPrimaryRepo: The public repo remains the primary collaboration repo for source, docs, tools, tests, RE notes, wave notes, state batons, agent reports, readiness notes, and compact proof summaries that are non-secret and non-payload.

coordinationPolicyIntegration.scope: durable coordination policy plus canonical goal/state reconciliation

currentTruth.claimBoundary: Coordination closeout and next-baton docs/state only; no product source, product tests, runtime behavior, patch semantics, launch behavior, safe-copy behavior, Host/Join capability, runtime proof, release/publication action, Ghidra mutation, hard-payload boundary, or installed-game/original-BEA mutation changed.
currentTruth.productBaseline: WinUI 3 remains the primary app, AppCore remains shared correctness logic, the C# CLI remains a support lane, and Python tooling remains repo/lab support.

runtimeBoundaries.onlineMultiplayer: not player-ready; Host/Join remains disabled until required distinct-endpoint and source-bound copied-runtime proof exists
runtimeBoundaries.staticReClosure: static closure is not runtime, gameplay, visual, online, rebuild, or parity proof

currentTruth.hardPayloadBoundary: Hard payloads, raw proof logs, screenshots/frame dumps, copied executable output, full Ghidra databases, secrets, .env files, local config, caches, and build/package output stay out of git and release ZIPs; use LOCAL_LAB_OVERLAY.md for local-only placement.

activeSlice.name: post-consolidation-repo-quality-modularity-stale-doc-cleanup
activeSlice.goal: from consolidated main, perform a read-first scan and choose one bounded repo-quality, modularity, or stale-doc cleanup with clear ownership and matching validation
activeSlice.claimBoundary: docs/state/repo-quality first; product code only after fresh file review and clear lease; no runtime, release, Host/Join, online, Ghidra, hard-payload, installed-game, or original-BEA claim changes

validation.closeout: Closeout validation results live in the local campaign report for coordination-branch-closeout-001.
validation.nextSlice: Use docs/state gates for documentation-only cleanup and add the relevant WinUI/AppCore gates before claiming any code or UI behavior change.

nextSteps: After main contains the accepted coordination closeout, start from clean main and re-read current front-door docs, goal.md, and state batons.
Use rg to find stale acceptance/campaign wording, outdated state pointers, and recent Windowed & Mods modularity follow-up candidates.
Select exactly one narrow path family with clear ownership before editing.

Keep patch rows, launch behavior, safe-copy behavior, music, online gates, runtime proof, release packaging, and installed-game mutation rules out of scope unless fresh evidence reveals a bounded bug.
